content: Well, since this dinner was so stressful, I just felt like everyone was thinking the same thing about me. They started asking questions anyway because I didn’t want them to notice how silly or weird I sounded. And then, when I tried something different or wasn’t involved enough, I ended up laughing and agreeing to things that weren’t really what I wanted. It’s like I’m always trying to prove myself too much, and then they all just leave. Honestly, I feel like maybe I’m stuck somewhere between being interesting and annoying. It’s hard to stop smiling and nodding even though I know it won’t help anyone. I guess I just don’t feel like I can stand out enough. So, I try harder to fit in, but it’s exhausting.

Yeah, this week has been really tough for me. When my partner starts talking again, I just feel like they’re trying to get me to change or maybe even end things. I keep thinking, “Why do they stay? Why don’t they just want to connect?” It’s exhausting to know that I’m not part of something important. Sometimes I just shut down completely, feeling trapped and unsure why they’re staying away. I guess I’ve always worried that if I show them how much I care, they’ll leave me too. It makes me feel small and useless, which only reinforces my feeling of being out of control. I don’t want to make them upset, though—I just want them to understand I need someone close. It’s frustrating because I want to help them feel better, but I’m afraid it’ll just prove they don’t love me anymore. So, I end up pulling back or pretending everything’s okay to avoid making things worse. It’s hard to break free when I see all the ways I’m failing to be a good person. I appreciate it when they stop, but it just makes me feel even more invisible.

Yeah, well, lately, whenever I’m given that big project, I just feel like I’m falling apart. I keep thinking, “What if everyone realizes I can’t handle this?” And honestly, when I try to tackle it alone, I end up staying stuck because it just feels too much to handle. It’s exhausting, and I don’t want anyone around me to notice how behind I am. Sometimes I even start hiding out from work because I’m afraid I’ll show up looking silly or embarrassing myself. I guess I just don’t trust myself to succeed, and I’m scared of being seen as inadequate. It makes me feel like maybe I’m never really good enough for the job. I wish I could just push through and say, "I’m sorry, but I’m not ready yet." But then again, I know it’s probably better to stay away from the burden altogether.

Hey therapist, last week was really rough trying to get invited to that little party. I kept thinking about everyone whispering and that I sounded silly, like I wasn’t quite ready yet. Even when I tried to join in, I found myself being extra polite and agreeing too much because I didn’t want to seem awkward or out of place. I ended up staying late at work and leaving early because I just couldn’t handle the pressure. It’s like I’m always falling short, and I feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of social stuff. I hope you can see why I’m having such hard times. Please let me know if I need to explain anything more. Thanks for being here. I appreciate you taking the time to talk to me.

Well, since then, I’ve been feeling like I’m stuck in this relationship, like I never really get to decide things for myself. It’s frustrating because when they start making choices, I just feel like I’m invisible, like I don’t belong anymore. I’m afraid if I try to speak up, maybe they’ll just push me away again. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I can’t escape whatever it is that makes me feel so helpless. I guess it’s just easier to just agree to what doesn’t seem right than to face up to what I know won’t work out for us. It’s exhausting, honestly. I want to change, but every time I try, it just feels like I’m failing. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where nothing changes. So, I’m just kind of going along with everything because it feels safer, even though I’m already feeling pretty scared. It’s exhausting, and I don’t think anyone wants to see me struggle like this.

Well, lately at the family gathering, it just felt like everything was going to fall apart when they started talking about different careers and lifestyles that didn’t really match what I wanted. I kept thinking, “There’s no way out,” and honestly, I felt pretty useless. When they got too deep into the discussion, I just kind of retreated, because I wasn’t sure if I could keep up or make any difference. It’s frustrating because I want to express my ideas, but then I just end up quiet and passive. I guess I’m scared that if I show my true feelings, people will see how flawed I am. It makes me feel like maybe I’m stuck in this never-ending loop where I can’t escape or change anything. I’ve noticed that whenever things get rough, I tend to stay away from things that were previously fun or interesting. It’s exhausting, and I feel like I’m losing touch with who I am.

Yeah, this weekend was really tough for me. When they started talking about my career and what I wanted to achieve, I just felt like I had no idea what to say or even if I’d ever actually want to change things. I kept thinking, “What if I’m just making things worse?” Or maybe there’s something wrong with me that makes me always feel like I’m falling short. After that, I spent hours replaying everything, feeling stuck and hopeless. It’s like I’m trapped inside that world that’s pushing me towards failure. Honestly, I guess I feel like I’m being pushed out of the family because I’m trying too hard to fit in. It’s exhausting, and I just want to get away from it all. I wish I could talk about it more openly, but it’s harder than I expect.

Yeah, so last week was kind of like a wave crashing on our relationship. My partner kept saying something wasn’t right, but instead of being honest, I just agreed with everything because I didn’t want to upset them or make them feel bad. It felt like I was letting them down, and then when I tried to say something differently, they just shut me down again. Honestly, I’m scared that if I speak up, it’ll just confirm that I don’t belong here anymore. It’s like no matter what I say, it’s just going to end in failure or disappointment. So I’ve been trying to suppress my own needs and wants, even though I know they’re probably hurting them anyway. It’s frustrating because I want to be strong and independent, but every time I push back, it just pushes them away. I guess I just don’t know how to stop pretending to be okay, which makes me feel really ashamed. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m afraid to step out of it until I can convince them to change their mind.

Well, the family gathering was… well, it felt like no one really wanted to talk to me. I kept noticing everyone chatting and laughing together, but I didn’t feel included at all. It’s like I’m the only one there, and I just feel invisible or not important enough. I guess I’ve been feeling like maybe I’m not good enough for them. It makes me want to disappear, even though I know it won’t help. I prefer to just be somewhere else, maybe playing video games or doing something less socialistic. It’s exhausting to be stuck here. I’m sorry if this feels like a recurring pattern, but I haven’t figured out how to stop feeling this way lately. It’s hard to connect with people when I’m already feeling so distant.

Yeah, last week was really tough when I looked at my budget and realized I couldn’t even pay my rent or put food on the table. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t make enough money next month? I’m always falling short.” It’s like I’m constantly failing, no matter how hard I try. I’ve tried to look for jobs online, but honestly, I’m scared of making mistakes that show everyone else that I’m a failure. Sometimes I just shut down completely, avoiding any discussions around my financial struggles because I’m afraid to admit I’m stuck. It’s exhausting to keep pushing through, but then I feel worse than before, like I’m never going to get back on track. I guess I just don’t trust myself to handle these things alone anymore. It’s like I need someone to tell me I’m wrong or that I’m failing.

Yeah, so last week, I got the news that I had to apply for a promotion at work, and honestly, I felt like all these little things were starting to weigh down on me. I kept thinking, “What if I mess up? Everyone will see how stupid I am.” And then, I stopped even trying because it felt safer not to try anything new. I guess I’m afraid of being seen as not good enough, especially since everyone seems to care less than I do about what I do. It’s frustrating because I want to prove myself, but somehow, no matter how hard I try, I still slip up. I keep thinking maybe I’ll never get that promotion. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m always getting pushed away, but I’m not really sure why. I’d prefer not to talk about it because it makes me feel worse, but then again, hiding something embarrassing isn’t helping either. So, I guess I’m scared to ask for help or admit I need support because it just feels like asking for more won’t make me better. It’s like, why give myself permission to let go of myself when there’s already a lot going on?

Yeah, it’s like when the boss says we need to coordinate something really big and complicated right away, and then I just think “Nope.” I’ve tried to tackle parts of it early, but every time I get bogged down, it makes me feel like maybe I’m failing at everything. Honestly, I just want to put off what’s happening so I don’t seem so bad or look foolish to everyone else. It’s exhausting because it’s like I’m trapped inside myself and there’s no way out. Sometimes I wonder why I even bother trying—why bother looking forward to all the things I’ll accomplish? It’s frustrating and overwhelming at the same time. I guess I just don’t know how to let go of feeling helpless anymore. It feels like I’m always putting on a show, but then I realize it’s just not enough.

Well, um, during that lunch meeting, I felt like everyone was just staring at me. I kept trying to join in, but it just made me feel like I wasn’t really part of anything. I ended up leaving early because I just couldn’t keep up with them or feel like I was being ignored or dismissed. It’s like no matter what I say or how interested I might be, people just don’t seem to care about me anymore. It makes me question if I’m actually interesting or special. Honestly, I’ve been feeling this way since then, and it’s hard to shake off the feeling that I’m never going to change or become someone valued. I guess I just don’t want to face anyone again and I’m scared of losing them all. It’s like they’re saying, “You’re not worth keeping” or something similar. So, I guess I need to start feeling less alone here and maybe ask for help, but I’m afraid it’ll just prove how useless I really am. I’m sorry if this feels uncomfortable, but I’m just tired of feeling like I’m not good enough. It’s okay to doubt yourself sometimes, but I also need some reassurance that I’m important. Please let me know if there’s anything I could use to feel better. I appreciate you being here, but I’m so sorry that this happened to you. You’re doing great, and I’m glad you’re still around. I’ll remember this experience and try harder next time. Thank you for understanding. I hope you understand why I’m struggling.

Yeah, last week, when my partner started complaining about how much time I was wasting at work, I just felt this overwhelming pressure to stop what I was doing. I kept thinking, “What if I’m just making things worse?” Then, I quickly said sorry, canceled some plans, and even tried to be extra attentive because I didn’t want them to feel like they were judging me or that I wasn’t good enough. It’s hard to stop from worrying that maybe they’re just thinking I’m being needy or that I’m not strong enough to handle relationships properly. Honestly, I’ve been struggling to connect and express my feelings without getting caught up in all the drama. It’s like I’m always having to prove myself again and again. I guess I just don’t trust my instincts anymore around people. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I need someone to understand me better. But I don’t know if I can really help fix anything without making them upset. So, it feels easier to just pretend everything is okay and let go.

Well, lately, I’ve just felt like I’m falling apart when I’m trying to lead something big. It’s like everyone notices how little I know already, and then there’s this nagging feeling that I’m failing right away. I’ve tried to pull out and just stay in the comfort zone, but even that feels exhausting. Plus, I’m so anxious about showing off, which makes it hard to keep things under control. Honestly, I’m terrified that if I mess up, people will think less of me and maybe leave me behind. It’s like I’m trapped in this never-ending loop of fear and disappointment. I guess I just don’t feel ready for what’s ahead. It’s like I’m stuck between hoping and fearing that I’ll fall short and lose everything.

Yeah, um, during our dinner last night, my partner suggested I go somewhere I don’t really care about. I ended up agreeing, even though I was tired and already looking forward to going elsewhere. It felt kind of awkward, like no matter what I said, people were still judging me or thinking less of me. I kept thinking, “If I didn’t agree, maybe they’ll stop liking me.” It’s hard to say, because I’m scared of making things worse or getting hurt again. Honestly, I just wanted to avoid causing any trouble or feeling like I wasn’t important enough. I feel like I’m letting everyone down if I’m not there for them. It’s exhausting knowing that when I need someone close, I can’t seem to keep up. I guess I just don’t believe in myself sometimes, which makes me feel like I’m failing. It’s frustrating because I want to be around people who value me, but I’m afraid of disappointing them or losing them. I wish I could change things, but I’m too afraid of hurting anyone. So, it’s mostly just a constant fear of failure or rejection.

Well, this past weekend was really tough. When my parents started criticizing my career choice and lifestyle, I just felt like nothing was getting better. It’s like I’m always falling short, and no matter what I say, it doesn’t feel like enough. I ended up leaving early because I didn’t want to continue being a part of this feeling. Honestly, I guess I’m just scared to show anyone how much I miss my own life. It’s exhausting to keep pretending things aren’t bad, even though they’re clearly there. I don’t know why I keep avoiding the whole thing—maybe it’s because it makes me feel worse already? It’s hard to connect with people again after something so big happened. I’ll stop talking until I get home and figure out what I need to say.

Yeah, last week was really tough because when my partner talked about going to some distant family gatherings, I just kept thinking, “What if they don’t like me there? What if they’re too much?” So, I tried to change things around, suggesting they could stay at home instead, but then I ended up being even more accommodating, hoping maybe it wouldn’t happen again. It’s like, whenever I try to keep things simple, they just decide to move forward anyway. Honestly, it just makes me feel worse—like no matter what I do, it’ll never work out the way I wanted. I guess I’m just scared that if I ever have to deal with this again, I’ll break down and say sorry. It’s exhausting, but I’m afraid it won’t stop them from loving me away.

Yeah, this past week has really been overwhelming. When I’m given something new at work, I get so anxious already, and then I just freeze and double-check everything again and again. It feels like I’m always missing something important or someone is judging me too much. Sometimes, I just want to skip classes or put off tasks because I don’t know what’s going to happen next. Honestly, it makes me feel like maybe I’m not cut out for life in the workplace. I’ve tried to pull myself away from work, but it just leads to another setback or worse. It’s like I’m trapped in this never-ending cycle where I can’t change anything or feel better. I guess I just feel scared of failing and being seen as unworthy. It’s hard to connect with people around me, and I worry they’ll think less of me. So, I usually stay behind behind the scenes or pretend things aren’t happening, even if I’m exhausted. It’s exhausting and makes me want to give up on trying harder. I’m sorry if this seems like I’m not thinking deeply about these feelings, but I feel like I’m stuck in this endless loop.

Yeah, the dinner was really tough for me. When my parents mentioned my sibling’s new business, it just made me feel like I’m invisible, like no one really cares about how much I’m contributing. I ended up staying late because I couldn’t handle being around people who were all talking about my brother. It felt exhausting and like I wasn’t helping myself. Honestly, I guess I just don’t know how to make things better, even though I know I need to. It makes me feel like maybe I’m failing at something important, which is hard to shake. Sometimes, I wonder if I’m actually just a weak kid who can’t stand up for myself. I hope they’ll let me go, but honestly, it’s harder than I imagined. I don’t want to cause any more stress or hurt, but I’m scared I’ll embarrass myself too much. It’s like I’m stuck between feeling powerless and somehow not powerful enough.

Yeah, the invitation came late last night, and honestly, I just couldn’t shake this feeling of being too small or not wanted there. I kept thinking, “Everyone’s going to see how awkward I am when I’m there,” and then, instead of saying something, I ended up telling myself I’d better go somewhere else because I don’t want to be part of what they’re talking about. It’s like I’m trying really hard not to mess up, but every time I try, it just seems easier to stay home. I feel so guilty about not being able to connect or help someone, and then I wonder if maybe they already didn’t need me anyway. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like I’m just going to fall apart again. I guess I’m scared of making things worse or showing them I’m not worth their time or attention. It’s frustrating because I want to be around them, but I’m always afraid of disappointing them or getting left behind.

Yeah, the dinner was pretty rough for me. My parents just kept praising the sibling while I barely made any mention of my own achievements. It’s like they’re all waiting for me to fall down and show them how unworthy I am. I’ve been thinking a lot lately because when someone talks about their successes, I shut down, and then I end up just being so ashamed inside. It makes me feel like maybe I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff at home. I guess I just don’t want to be left behind anymore. Sometimes it feels easier to stay quiet than face the truth. It’s frustrating because I know I’m doing something wrong anyway. But I can’t shake this feeling that I’m just too insignificant. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out where these feelings are coming from. Thanks!

Yeah, so… When my friends said to join the community group, I just felt like I was letting everyone down. They were saying something new, but I didn’t really want to try because I worried they’d notice how awkward I’m being. It’s like if I say anything different, people’ll think less of me or won’t even invite me back. So instead, I stayed quiet and pretended not to care, even when I had an idea or wanted to contribute. Honestly, it made me feel kind of lonely and invisible too. It’s frustrating because I know I should be trying harder to make connections anyway. But then again, maybe it’s better to stay safe than risk getting rejected all over again. I guess I just don’t feel ready to be a part of this community yet.

Yeah, the get-together was… kind of hard for me. When people start talking without me saying anything, it just felt like no one really cares about what I have to say. I kept thinking maybe they’re not even paying attention because I wasn’t there. And then I just ended up leaving early because I couldn’t keep going, and I’m still feeling pretty sad afterwards. It’s like I feel invisible or like nothing matters except how I act around them. Honestly, whenever I think about what went wrong, it makes me feel even worse about myself. It’s frustrating because I want to connect and contribute, but sometimes it’s easier to stay out of the middle. I guess I just don’t trust people much, so whatever happens is okay. Sometimes I wonder if anyone really thinks I’m valuable. I hope they can see that I need help and support instead of just dismissing me. It’s tough being part of a team where everyone seems to want different things than mine.

Well, this whole project just feels like a huge weight on my shoulders. When I first started talking about it, I felt like maybe I was missing out because everyone seemed so confident about what I was doing. I kept thinking, “What if they don’t want me around? Or maybe they’re already realizing I’m not cut out for this.” So, I ended up being super careful, trying to hide every little detail I noticed, even though it made things harder. It’s exhausting, really—it makes me feel like I’m failing somewhere or that no one wants me around. I guess I just don’t trust myself enough in these situations. It’s like I’m always afraid I’ll mess everything up again. I’d rather not be there. It’s hard to keep pushing forward, especially when I know it won’t change anything.

Hey therapist, I’ve been feeling like this again. The bank bill isn’t even showing up, and I’m starting to get really anxious. It feels like nothing matters because I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I feel like I’m failing, then maybe something else happens. I keep putting off paying it because I don’t want to deal with the stress or look like a failure. It’s exhausting, and I just end up avoiding dealing with anything financially since I’m afraid I’ll screw things up again. Honestly, I feel like I’m just not cut out to handle money well. It’s like no matter how hard I try, it always fails. I guess I’m just plain terrified that if I mess up financially, it’ll show everyone that I’m not capable. Please let me know if you think I need to talk about these feelings more. I’d appreciate any ideas you have! Thanks a ton.

Oh, well, yeah, the friend came over and then… I felt like maybe they weren’t actually really interested in me after all. It’s like, why do I even bother showing up? When I got close, it just made me feel worse, and honestly, I don’t know what to do anymore. I ended up leaving early because it felt safer than staying here and feeling all alone again. It’s like this person is just proving how much I’m meant to be left behind, and I’m stuck somewhere between hoping they’ll change or get hurt. It makes me feel kind of hopeless and sad inside. I guess I just don’t want to deal with the whole thing happening again. It’s hard to explain to anyone—especially to someone who cares about me.

Yeah, the family meeting was really tough for me. I kept feeling like I had no say in what happened, just like I don’t get to decide anything on my own. I guess I’m afraid if I voice my ideas, it’ll just come back to them and feel like I’m failing. It’s exhausting trying so hard to fit in but never truly getting what I wanted. After avoiding it again, I just feel worse and kind of shut down completely. I don’t know how to stop thinking about those meetings—like maybe I’m just too vulnerable or invisible there. It’s like when I’m being watched all the time, even though I know I’m okay. I feel like everyone wants to be the one deciding what happens, which makes me feel like I’m letting them down. It’s exhausting to keep pretending to be different from everyone else, but then I end up feeling even more alone. I hope they find out why I’m having such a tough time. It’s frustrating because I want to be part of whatever changes happen, but I’m just stuck in this loop.

Yeah, it all started when I got my bank statement yesterday. It showed that I had another $100 in debt, and honestly, I just froze up. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t fix this? Everyone will notice how struggling.” I didn’t want to talk about it because it seemed too personal and scary, so instead, I just stayed quiet and avoided looking at the bill or following up. Honestly, I feel like I’m drowning in these feelings of failure and guilt. It’s hard to connect with people when I’m constantly worried about losing them. I guess I just don’t know how to start things right. It makes me question if I really deserve someone’s love or care. I wish there was some way to say enough without feeling completely rejected. It’s exhausting sometimes, but I’ve been trying to push through, hoping it’ll get better. I don’t know, I guess I need to find a healthier way to deal with this than bury myself in sadness and fear.

Yeah, this situation really hits home. When I started dating someone new, I felt like I had to keep everything perfect, or else they’d think I’m not cut out for them. I’ll go out sometimes, pretend to like what they say, but deep inside, I’m afraid I’ll mess things up or seem weak. It’s exhausting because I want to be there for them, but also scared that I’ll lose them. I guess I feel like maybe if I just showed up authentically, they wouldn’t want to stay. It’s hard to escape those recurring thoughts and worries when I’m already so anxious about myself. I’m sorry if these feelings come off as blaming or unkind— honestly, I don’t know how to stop thinking like that. Sometimes I just freeze up and don’t respond at all. It’s frustrating because I want to help them grow and deepen our connection, but I’m stuck in a loop of trying too hard. I appreciate it when I’m reminded that being imperfect isn’t a reflection on me—it’s just reality.

Oh, so this week was like a rollercoaster of pressure. When I got the big project coming, I just felt overwhelmed, like no matter what I tried, it was too much. I kept thinking, “I’m just not cut out for this,” which made me feel even more ashamed about being able to handle things efficiently. Even though I knew it wasn’t going to go smoothly, I kept putting myself under too much stress to try and keep up. It’s exhausting—like I’m constantly falling behind everyone else because I don’t seem to know enough to take care of things properly. I guess I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m trying harder than I should, hoping it’ll pay off, but it never does. It’s frustrating, but also overwhelming sometimes. I wish I could just relax and focus on something less stressful, but I know I can’t. It’s like I’m always struggling to prove I’m capable when I’m actually failing. So, I’ve ended up staying late almost every night and skipping breakfast to make sure I’m okay, even though it’s killing me mentally. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t want to bring this on anyone else. I guess I just don’t believe in my own abilities. It’s like I’m stuck in this never-ending loop where I’m already failing anyway. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m being honest, but I don’t know why I’m having these thoughts anymore. It’s hard to stop feeling like I’m not good enough, and I’m scared I’ll never get better. I hope you see how hard it must be for me to deal with this kind of pressure. Thank you for asking, I really need some help. It’s making me feel worse. I appreciate you seeing me through this.

Yeah, last week was really tough. When they gave me the new project, I just felt like everything was falling apart, like I couldn’t keep up even though I’ve practiced a lot. I kept thinking, “Why bother? It’ll all come out okay eventually.” I called in sick almost every day because I didn’t want to mess up anything. Honestly, I guess I just wanted to avoid being seen as a failure or letting people know I’m not cut out for this job. It’s like no matter what I try, it always ends up feeling like a failure anyway. I feel trapped, like there’s no escape path from these projects. It’s exhausting, and I’m scared I’ll never get back to where I used to be. I don’t know if I can ever trust myself to succeed in these big things. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle of trying harder than I need to, then failing miserably. I guess I’m scared of disappointing anyone, or maybe I’m just too afraid to ask for help. It’s frustrating because knowing I’m struggling already makes me want to give up. But honestly, I don’t know why I’m so resistant to change. It’s like I’m stuck in a place where nothing feels right anymore. So, I guess I’m asking for permission to stop playing this game and maybe start fresh. Maybe talking to someone about it could help me find some space or reassurance. But I’m not sure if that’ll make a difference. It’s like I’m drowning in my own fear of failure.

Yeah, I got that notice about the bills being late and some money left in my bank account. Honestly, every time I check, it just seems like no matter what I try, it just gets harder. I’m afraid of not being good enough, or maybe even failing again. Sometimes I just skip paying the bill because it’s easier than dealing with all the stress of figuring things out myself. It’s exhausting, and I don’t want to make things worse by putting myself further behind. I guess I’ve been thinking about trying to save up for something big, but it’s hard to get started because I’m scared everything will fall apart. It’s like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop where I keep getting knocked down instead of standing up. I’d rather pretend everything was okay than face the pain of admitting defeat. It’s frustrating because I know deep inside I need to fix this, but I’m just too scared to move forward.

Yeah, this whole project came really fast on me. I kept thinking, “This thing is going to take forever,” and then when I started working on it, it felt like no matter how hard I tried, I couldn’t even get a little bit done. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone about it because I’m scared I’ll embarrass myself or sound stupid. It’s exhausting—like I’m always running from a big fight. Sometimes I just want to shut down and forget about it, but then I feel even worse afterward. Honestly, I don’t know what to expect from them. It’s like everyone sees my weaknesses, so I’m afraid they’ll judge me as less capable. I guess I just don’t trust myself enough to tackle tough stuff alone. It’s exhausting, but also frustrating because it feels like no one wants me around unless I prove I’m competent. I wish I could talk to someone about it and maybe find some way out of this. But honestly, I’m just scared to try. It’s like I’m never going to get anywhere with this project.

I’m so… embarrassed. When they’re gone, it’s like no matter how much I say or how much I try, they just seem to want things back again. I’ve tried to tell them they’re tired of me, but it doesn’t really make them change their mind. Sometimes, I just feel like I’m invisible, like they recognize how much I’m hurting instead of loving me. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know why I even bother trying anymore. I guess I just feel like I’m failing at everything. It’s like I’m always missing out on the moments when we were close together. I don’t want to cause them to think differently; it’s easier than feeling like I’m not good enough. So, I end up just sending more messages hoping they’ll stop noticing me, but it only makes me feel worse. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle of never knowing what happens next. I don’t know, I guess I just don’t trust myself to be okay. It’s frustrating because I want to connect and be loved, but sometimes it feels like I’m just not cut out for life.
